name: Survodutide
subtitle: Dual GLP-1/Glucagon Receptor Agonist | Weight Loss & Diabetes

modifications: C18 fatty acid acylation for extended half-life (~6 days)

overview: Investigational dual receptor agonist targeting metabolic disease through balanced GLP-1R and GCGR activation. Phase 2/3 clinical trials demonstrate superior weight loss and MASH treatment efficacy.
mechanism: Dual agonism: GLP-1R reduces appetite and slows gastric emptying; GCGR increases energy expenditure and hepatic fat oxidation. EC50 0.52nM GCGR, 0.33nM GLP-1R.
keyBenefits: Superior weight loss vs monotherapy (14.9% at 46 weeks)
Proven efficacy in obesity, MASH, and Type 2 diabetes

items.name: Obesity Without Diabetes
items.description: 14.9% mean weight loss at 46 weeks (4.8mg); 55% achieved ≥15% reduction.

items.name: Obesity With Type 2 Diabetes
items.description: Superior to semaglutide: -8.7% vs -5.3% at 16 weeks.

items.name: Sustained Weight Management
items.description: Dual mechanism addresses both energy intake and expenditure.

items.name: MASH Treatment
items.description: 62% achieved MASH improvement without fibrosis worsening at 4.8mg.

items.name: Type 2 Diabetes Control
items.description: HbA1c reduction up to -1.6% at highest doses.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection to abdomen (2+ inches from navel), upper thigh, or upper arm. Any time of day, with or without food.

steps: Remove vial from refrigerator; allow 15-30 minutes to reach room temperature
Clean rubber stoppers with alcohol swabs
Draw prescribed amount of bacteriostatic water
Insert needle at 45-degree angle into vial
Inject BAC water down vial side slowly to minimize foaming
Solution should be clear to slightly opalescent without particles
Store reconstituted solution in refrigerator immediately

interactions.notes: Both GLP-1 agonists; combining risks excessive activation and GI side effects.
Demonstrated together in Phase 2 diabetes trials.
No known interactions; allowed in Phase 3 with monitoring.
May require insulin dose reduction due to improved glycemic control.
Take 1+ hour before survodutide due to delayed gastric emptying.
Risk of excessive glucagon receptor activation.

timeline.effects: Possible nausea, reduced appetite (most common during escalation)
Initial weight loss begins; improved satiety between meals
Progressive weight loss; potential energy level improvements
Sustained weight loss; average 15-19% by week 46

stopSigns: Severe persistent nausea/vomiting preventing oral intake
Signs of pancreatitis (severe abdominal pain radiating to back)
contraindications: Not recommended in pregnancy or breastfeeding
monitoring: Monitor blood glucose if on diabetes medications
No dose adjustment needed for hepatic impairment (including cirrhosis)
Monitor heart rate in patients with cardiac conditions

good: Clear to slightly opalescent solution without visible particles
Sealed vial with intact rubber stopper
warning: Slight foam after reconstitution is normal if disappears within minutes
bad: Cloudy solution or visible particles indicates contamination

references.title: Phase 2 Obesity Trial Without Diabetes
references.keyFindings: 387 participants; BMI ≥27 kg/m²; 46 weeks. 14.9% mean weight loss at 4.8mg; 83% achieved ≥5%, 69% achieved ≥10%, 55% achieved ≥15%.

references.title: Phase 2 MASH and Fibrosis Trial
references.keyFindings: 293 participants with biopsy-confirmed MASH; 48 weeks. MASH improvement: 62% (4.8mg) vs 14% placebo; 63-67% achieved ≥30% liver fat reduction.

references.title: Phase 2 Type 2 Diabetes Trial
references.keyFindings: Head-to-head vs semaglutide 1.0mg; 16 weeks. Survodutide -8.7% weight loss vs semaglutide -5.3%; HbA1c reduction up to -1.6%.
